info.title: EventHorizon.Blazor.TypeScript.Interop.Tool:
info.description: Generate a Blazor Wasm or Server project from TypeScript definition files.

options.name: --source
options.description: TypeScript Definition to generate from, can be a File or URL, accepts multiple or as Array

options.name: --class-to-generate
options.description: A Class to generate, accepts multiple or as Array

options.name: --project-assembly
options.description: The project name of the Assembly that will be generated [default: Generated.Code]

options.name: --project-generation-location
options.description: The directory where the Generated Project assembly will be saved [default: _generated]

options.name: --force
options.description: This will force generation, by deleting --project-generation-location [default: False]

options.name: --parser
options.description: The type of TypeScript parser to use, 'dotnet' will use the embedded .NET parser. 'nodejs' will use the TypeScript compiler through NodeJS (requires NodeJS installed). [default: dotnet]

options.name: --host-type
options.description: The host type the source should be generator for. Is 'wasm' by default, will only work for Wasm hosted Blazor projects. Supports 'server' to generate a async first interop layer that works in both Blazor Server and Wasm hosted models. [default: wasm]
